message.content:
POLARIS SPEED-VS-QUALITY INVESTIGATION. Established facts (measured today):
- A deep report render (route_all = 328 of 329 baskets) takes ~30+ min END-TO-END, and the AGENTIC
  RETRIEVAL phase (the react loop fetching URLs one turn at a time) dominates (~24 min); the COMPOSE
  phase is the smaller slice.
- We ported compose_fix's 16-way concurrency into outline_agent. The OFF-LOOP to_thread wrap is
  verdict-safe. But the aggressive PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS + semaphore(48) DEADLOCKED at full
  328-basket scale: 19/20 threads stuck in futex_wait, 0 progress 8.8min, had to SIGKILL. The small
  verdict-identity A/B passed but never exercised full-scale concurrency.
- Also observed: the outline can DEGRADE to seed+partial under load (must stay cp4_used=agentic).

QUALITY INVARIANTS ANY SPEEDUP MUST KEEP (non-negotiable):
1. DEEP COVERAGE: render ~328/329 baskets (route_all). Do NOT speed up by dropping baskets/coverage.
2. FAITHFULNESS: strict_verify gate intact; kept/dropped NLI verdict set unchanged; no unverified/
   derived number renders via [CITE:ev_xxx]. Speed must be verdict-identical.
3. AGENTIC: cp4_used=agentic, never degrade-to-seed as a shortcut.
4. NO DEADLOCK: a speedup that can hang is not a speedup.

READ-ONLY: read code in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent and /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix; run cheap
deterministic profiling; write findings ONLY to /home/polaris/polaris_project/speed_investigation.md.
Do NOT edit either worktree (a wheel is live in outline_agent). Source key for any measured run:
set -a; . /workspace/POLARIS/.env; set +a. Report REAL measured numbers; if you can't measure, say so.


ADVERSARIALLY VERIFY this proposed speedup. Does it REALLY save meaningful wall-time AND keep ALL 4 quality invariants (deep coverage, faithfulness verdict-identity, cp4_used=agentic, no-deadlock)? Default to keeps_quality=FALSE if it risks any invariant or the saving is marginal.

SPEEDUP: Cache the fetched BYTES only (keyed by canonicalized DOI/PMID/URL); keep strict_verify + NLI running LIVE on cached content so the verdict is recomputed fresh, never cached. Wire eviction-on-retraction.
CLAIMED SAVING: Run-dependent; largest on re-runs / overlapping queries. Zero on a cold single run.
STATED RISK: I2 (faithfulness): stale cached source can keep a claim whose evidence was retracted -> mitigated by eviction API (not pure TTL) and by recomputing the verdict live. Cache-key canonicalization must not merge two DIFFERENT sources. Coverage-neutral (I1) and cannot degrade (I3). SAFE IF GUARDED.
EVIDENCE: retrieval_cache.py header: per-workspace SQLite cache with eviction API (evict_by_url, evict_older_than); module EXPLICITLY rejects pure TTL because a retracted/superseded source is a faithfulness liability.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""M-D7 (Phase D): Retrieval cache — bootstrap.
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN M-D7 + Phase D milestones plan: aggressive
4	caching of source-fetch responses (CrossRef / Unpaywall /
5	PubMed / canonical-URL fetches). Reuses the M-21 SQLite
6	workspace substrate — one DB file per workspace, one extra
7	table for cached fetches.
8	
9	Phase 1 ships **per-workspace cache + explicit eviction API**.
10	Phase 2 (system-wide cache + empirical ≥80% time-saved
11	acceptance test) is deferred.
12	
13	## Why per-workspace (not global) for phase 1
14	
15	Global cache adds auth/isolation complexity that doesn't belong
16	in a perf milestone. M-21 already establishes the per-workspace
17	SQLite substrate — extending it with one more table is the
18	narrowest cut. M-D7 phase 2 can promote to global once the
19	isolation contract is designed (likely with M-D13 collaboration
20	work).
21	
22	## Cache key strategy
23	
24	For academic sources, key on the persistent identifier:
25	  - DOI (lowercased, prefix-stripped)
26	  - PMID (digits only)
27	
28	For web-fetched URLs, normalize via the same helper M-16 uses
29	for run-diff URL canonicalization (`run_diff._normalize_url`):
30	  - lowercase netloc, strip `www.`
31	  - drop tracking params (utm_*, fbclid, etc.)
32	  - sort remaining params, drop fragment + trailing slash
33	  - drop scheme (http/https treated identically)
34	
35	This means `?utm_source=x&id=1` and `?id=1` hit the same cache
36	entry, and `https://Example.COM/` and `http://example.com`
37	match.
38	
39	## Eviction API (NOT pure TTL)
40	
41	Per advisor + Codex round-1 reasoning: pure TTL is
42	insufficient. Stale cached sources become a faithfulness
43	liability when the underlying source is retracted or
44	superseded. We expose:
45	
46	  - `evict(workspace_id, cache_key)` — remove one entry
47	  - `evict_by_url(workspace_id, source_url)` — convenience
48	    wrapper (canonicalizes the URL first)
49	  - `evict_older_than(workspace_id, max_age_seconds)` — bulk
50	    age-based eviction (the TTL primitive)
51	  - `evict_all(workspace_id)` — workspace-wide flush
52	
53	This shape lets M-D10 (citation freshness monitoring) hook in
54	later: when M-D10's daemon detects a retracted DOI, it calls
55	`evict_by_url(workspace_id, doi_url)` to invalidate the cache.
56	
57	## Coupling to M-21
58	
59	Same DB file, same isolation contract. The cache table is
60	independent of the memory table — they don't share rows or
61	foreign keys. A workspace can have memory entries without
62	cache entries and vice versa.
63	
64	## What's pinned in M-D11
65	
66	The cache content version is NOT in `ModelPin.retrieval_source_versions`
67	because the cache is workspace-scoped and the pin is run-scoped.
68	A workspace's cache state is captured at run time by the
69	workspace's existing audit trail (M-23 review queue records),
70	not by the model pin. Phase 2 may add a `cache_revision`
71	counter if replay needs bit-exact cache state.
72	"""
73	
74	from __future__ import annotations
75	
76	import hashlib
77	import re
78	import sqlite3
79	import time
80	import uuid
81	from dataclasses import dataclass
82	from pathlib import Path
83	from typing import Any
84	
85	
86	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
87	# Errors
88	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
89	
90	
91	class RetrievalCacheError(Exception):
92	    """Raised on schema/state violations."""
93	
94	
95	class RetrievalCacheStateError(RetrievalCacheError):
96	    """Raised when the store's connection is closed / unusable."""
97	
98	
99	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
100	# Data class
101	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
102	
103	
104	@dataclass(frozen=True)
105	class CacheEntry:
106	    """One cached source-fetch response.
107	
108	    `cache_key` is the canonicalized identifier (see
109	    `make_cache_key`). `payload` is the response body bytes —
110	    SQLite BLOB. `content_type` and `payload_sha256` let
111	    consumers verify integrity without re-fetching.
112	
113	    `fetched_at` is the timestamp of the underlying fetch (for
114	    age-based eviction). `last_hit_at` updates on every read,
115	    enabling LRU-style bulk eviction in phase 2.
116	    """
117	
118	    cache_key: str
119	    workspace_id: str
120	    source_url: str
121	    payload: bytes
122	    content_type: str
123	    payload_sha256: str
124	    fetched_at: float
125	    last_hit_at: float | None
126	    fetch_status_code: int
127	
128	
129	def cache_entry_to_dict(entry: CacheEntry) -> dict[str, Any]:
130	    """JSON-safe dict (without the binary payload)."""
131	    return {
132	        "cache_key": entry.cache_key,
133	        "workspace_id": entry.workspace_id,
134	        "source_url": entry.source_url,
135	        "content_type": entry.content_type,
136	        "payload_sha256": entry.payload_sha256,
137	        "payload_size_bytes": len(entry.payload),
138	        "fetched_at": entry.fetched_at,
139	        "last_hit_at": entry.last_hit_at,
140	        "fetch_status_code": entry.fetch_status_code,
141	    }

144	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
145	# Schema
146	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
147	
148	
149	_SCHEMA = """
150	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS retrieval_cache (
151	    cache_key TEXT NOT NULL,
152	    workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL,
153	    source_url TEXT NOT NULL,
154	    payload BLOB NOT NULL,
155	    content_type TEXT NOT NULL,
156	    payload_sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
157	    fetched_at REAL NOT NULL,
158	    last_hit_at REAL,
159	    fetch_status_code INTEGER NOT NULL,
160	    PRIMARY KEY (workspace_id, cache_key)
161	);
162	
163	-- Fast workspace-scoped age scans for evict_older_than().
164	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_retrieval_cache_ws_fetched
165	    ON retrieval_cache(workspace_id, fetched_at);
166	
167	-- Fast LRU scans (phase 2 will use this).
168	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_retrieval_cache_ws_hit
169	    ON retrieval_cache(workspace_id, last_hit_at);
170	"""
171	
172	
173	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
174	# Cache key construction
175	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
176	
177	
178	_DOI_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(
179	    r"^(?:https?://)?(?:dx\.)?doi\.org/", re.IGNORECASE
180	)
181	_PMID_RE = re.compile(
182	    r"^(?:https?://)?(?:www\.)?(?:ncbi\.nlm\.nih\.gov/pubmed/|pubmed\.ncbi\.nlm\.nih\.gov/)(\d+)/?$",
183	    re.IGNORECASE,
184	)
185	# DOI shape per Crossref + ANSI/NISO Z39.84-2010:
186	#   prefix: 10.<registrant 4-9 digits, optional dotted sub-prefixes>
187	#   suffix: any non-whitespace 1+ chars
188	# We additionally exclude `/`/`?`/`#`/whitespace from the suffix
189	# because those almost always indicate URL decoration we want to
190	# strip (or, in the `/` case, structural decoration we keep — see
191	# below).
192	_DOI_FULL_RE = re.compile(
193	    r"^10\.[0-9]{4,9}(?:\.[0-9]+)*/[^\s?#]+$"
194	)
195	
196	
197	def _canonicalize_doi(raw: str) -> str | None:
198	    """Normalize a DOI string. Returns the bare DOI (e.g.
199	    "10.1000/foo.bar") or None if input doesn't look like one.
200	
201	    Strips:
202	      - URL prefix (`https://doi.org/`, `dx.doi.org/`)
203	      - `doi:` scheme prefix
204	      - URL fragment (`#frag`)
205	      - URL query string (`?utm_source=x`)
206	      - trailing `/`
207	    Lower-cases the entire DOI (DOIs are
208	    case-insensitive per the standard).
209	    """
210	    if not raw:
211	        return None
212	    text = raw.strip().lower()
213	    text = _DOI_PREFIX_RE.sub("", text)
214	    if text.startswith("doi:"):
215	        # Tolerate `doi: 10.1000/foo` (space after colon) — some
216	        # citation styles emit this form.
217	        text = text[4:].lstrip()
218	    # Drop fragment first, then query.
219	    text = text.split("#", 1)[0]
220	    text = text.split("?", 1)[0]
221	    text = text.rstrip("/")
222	    if not text.startswith("10."):
223	        return None
224	    # Reject anything with whitespace.
225	    if any(c.isspace() for c in text):
226	        return None
227	    # Strict shape match — rejects `10.123/foo` (registrant must
228	    # be 4-9 digits) and other near-DOI strings like
229	    # `10.x` URL paths that happen to start with the prefix.
230	    if not _DOI_FULL_RE.match(text):
231	        return None
232	    return text
233	
234	
235	def _canonicalize_pmid(raw: str) -> str | None:
236	    """Normalize a PMID. Accepts URL forms and bare digits.
237	    Returns digits-only string or None."""
238	    if not raw:
239	        return None
240	    text = raw.strip()
241	    if text.isdigit():
242	        return text
243	    m = _PMID_RE.match(text)
244	    if m:
245	        return m.group(1)
246	    return None
247	
248	
249	def _normalize_web_url(url: str) -> str:
250	    """Reuse the M-16 URL canonicalizer. Importing lazily so
251	    this module's import surface stays tight."""
252	    from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.run_diff import _normalize_url
253	    return _normalize_url(url)
254	
255	
256	def make_cache_key(source_url: str) -> str:
257	    """Build a stable cache key from a source URL or identifier.
258	
259	    Tries DOI first (most stable), then PMID, then falls back to
260	    canonicalized web URL. Returns a string with a discriminator
261	    prefix so different identifier kinds never collide.
262	
263	    Raises RetrievalCacheError on empty input.
264	    """
265	    if not source_url or not source_url.strip():
266	        raise RetrievalCacheError("source_url must be non-empty")
267	    text = source_url.strip()
268	
269	    doi = _canonicalize_doi(text)
270	    if doi is not None:
271	        return f"doi:{doi}"
272	
273	    pmid = _canonicalize_pmid(text)
274	    if pmid is not None:
275	        return f"pmid:{pmid}"
276	
277	    normalized = _normalize_web_url(text)
278	    if not normalized:
279	        raise RetrievalCacheError(
280	            f"could not canonicalize source_url: {source_url!r}"
281	        )
282	    return f"url:{normalized}"
283	
284	
285	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
286	# Store
287	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
288	
289	
290	class RetrievalCacheStore:
291	    """SQLite-backed retrieval cache, per-workspace isolated.
292	
293	    Pattern matches M-21 `WorkspaceMemoryStore`: per-call
294	    connections, WAL journal, isolation_level=None for explicit
295	    transaction control. Cross-workspace isolation: every read +
296	    write requires a workspace_id. Same DB file as
297	    workspace_memory; one extra table.
298	    """
299	
300	    def __init__(self, db_path: Path | str) -> None:
301	        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
302	        self._db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
303	        self._init_schema()
304	
305	    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
306	        conn = sqlite3.connect(
307	            self._db_path, isolation_level=None, timeout=30.0,
308	        )
309	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
310	        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
311	        conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
312	        return conn
313	
314	    def _init_schema(self) -> None:
315	        with self._connect() as conn:
316	            conn.executescript(_SCHEMA)
317	
318	    # -- writes --
319	
320	    def put(
321	        self,
322	        *,
323	        workspace_id: str,
324	        source_url: str,
325	        payload: bytes,
326	        content_type: str,
327	        fetch_status_code: int,
328	        fetched_at: float | None = None,
329	        cache_key: str | None = None,
330	    ) -> CacheEntry:
331	        """Insert or replace a cache entry. Returns the persisted
332	        entry record.
333	
334	        Replaces on (workspace_id, cache_key) collision — refetch
335	        of the same source overwrites the stale payload.
336	        """
337	        if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
338	            raise RetrievalCacheError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
339	        if not isinstance(payload, bytes):
340	            raise RetrievalCacheError(
341	                f"payload must be bytes, got {type(payload).__name__}"
342	            )
343	        if not isinstance(fetch_status_code, int):
344	            raise RetrievalCacheError(
345	                "fetch_status_code must be int"
346	            )
347	
348	        key = cache_key if cache_key is not None else make_cache_key(source_url)
349	        ws = workspace_id.strip()
350	        ts = fetched_at if fetched_at is not None else time.time()
351	        sha = hashlib.sha256(payload).hexdigest()
352	        ctype = content_type.strip() if content_type else ""
353	
354	        with self._connect() as conn:
355	            conn.execute(
356	                """
357	                INSERT OR REPLACE INTO retrieval_cache
358	                    (cache_key, workspace_id, source_url, payload,
359	                     content_type, payload_sha256, fetched_at,
360	                     last_hit_at, fetch_status_code)
361	                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, NULL, ?)
362	                """,
363	                (key, ws, source_url, payload, ctype, sha, ts,
364	                 fetch_status_code),
365	            )
366	
367	        return CacheEntry(
368	            cache_key=key,
369	            workspace_id=ws,
370	            source_url=source_url,
371	            payload=payload,
372	            content_type=ctype,
373	            payload_sha256=sha,
374	            fetched_at=ts,
375	            last_hit_at=None,
376	            fetch_status_code=fetch_status_code,
377	        )
378	
379	    # -- reads --
380	
381	    def get(
382	        self, workspace_id: str, source_url: str
383	    ) -> CacheEntry | None:
384	        """Fetch by workspace + source URL. Returns None on miss.
385	
386	        Updates `last_hit_at` on hit (write-through, single
387	        UPDATE per read — kept cheap by the workspace-scoped
388	        index).
389	        """
390	        if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
391	            raise RetrievalCacheError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
392	        key = make_cache_key(source_url)
393	        return self._get_by_key(workspace_id.strip(), key)
394	
395	    def _get_by_key(
396	        self, workspace_id: str, cache_key: str
397	    ) -> CacheEntry | None:
398	        """Atomic SELECT + UPDATE last_hit_at.
399	
400	        Wrapped in BEGIN IMMEDIATE to prevent the race where a
401	        concurrent put()/evict() lands between SELECT and UPDATE
402	        — without the explicit transaction, the caller could
403	        receive a stale payload while last_hit_at lands on a
404	        newer or deleted row. BEGIN IMMEDIATE acquires the write
405	        lock at the start so no other writer can interleave.
406	        """
407	        with self._connect() as conn:
408	            conn.execute("BEGIN IMMEDIATE")
409	            try:
410	                cur = conn.execute(
411	                    "SELECT * FROM retrieval_cache "
412	                    "WHERE workspace_id=? AND cache_key=?",
413	                    (workspace_id, cache_key),
414	                )
415	                row = cur.fetchone()
416	                if row is None:
417	                    conn.execute("COMMIT")
418	                    return None
419	
420	                now = time.time()
421	                conn.execute(
422	                    "UPDATE retrieval_cache SET last_hit_at=? "
423	                    "WHERE workspace_id=? AND cache_key=?",
424	                    (now, workspace_id, cache_key),
425	                )
426	                conn.execute("COMMIT")
427	            except Exception:
428	                conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
429	                raise
430	        return _row_to_entry(row, last_hit_at_override=now)
431	
432	    # -- eviction (explicit, not pure TTL) --
433	
434	    def evict(self, workspace_id: str, cache_key: str) -> bool:
435	        """Remove one entry. Returns True if a row was deleted."""
436	        if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
437	            raise RetrievalCacheError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
438	        if not cache_key or not cache_key.strip():
439	            raise RetrievalCacheError("cache_key must be non-empty")
440	        with self._connect() as conn:
441	            cur = conn.execute(
442	                "DELETE FROM retrieval_cache "
443	                "WHERE workspace_id=? AND cache_key=?",
444	                (workspace_id.strip(), cache_key.strip()),
445	            )
446	            return cur.rowcount > 0
447	
448	    def evict_by_url(self, workspace_id: str, source_url: str) -> bool:
449	        """Convenience: canonicalize the URL then evict.
450	
451	        This is the API surface M-D10 (citation freshness
452	        monitoring) will call: when a DOI is detected as
453	        retracted/superseded, M-D10 invokes
454	        `evict_by_url(workspace_id, doi_url)` to invalidate the
455	        cache without needing to know our key format.
456	        """
457	        return self.evict(workspace_id, make_cache_key(source_url))
458	
459	    def evict_older_than(
460	        self, workspace_id: str, max_age_seconds: float
461	    ) -> int:
462	        """Bulk-evict entries older than `max_age_seconds`.
463	        Returns the number of rows deleted."""
464	        if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
465	            raise RetrievalCacheError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
466	        if max_age_seconds < 0:
467	            raise RetrievalCacheError(
468	                "max_age_seconds must be >=0"
469	            )
470	        cutoff = time.time() - max_age_seconds
471	        with self._connect() as conn:
472	            cur = conn.execute(
473	                "DELETE FROM retrieval_cache "
474	                "WHERE workspace_id=? AND fetched_at < ?",
475	                (workspace_id.strip(), cutoff),
476	            )
477	            return cur.rowcount
478	
479	    def evict_all(self, workspace_id: str) -> int:
480	        """Workspace-wide flush. Returns rows deleted."""
481	        if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
482	            raise RetrievalCacheError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
483	        with self._connect() as conn:
484	            cur = conn.execute(
485	                "DELETE FROM retrieval_cache WHERE workspace_id=?",
486	                (workspace_id.strip(),),
487	            )
488	            return cur.rowcount
489	
490	    # -- introspection --
491	
492	    def count(self, workspace_id: str) -> int:
493	        """Total entries in this workspace's cache."""
494	        if not workspace_id or not workspace_id.strip():
495	            raise RetrievalCacheError("workspace_id must be non-empty")
496	        with self._connect() as conn:
497	            cur = conn.execute(
498	                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM retrieval_cache "
499	                "WHERE workspace_id=?",
500	                (workspace_id.strip(),),
501	            )
502	            return cur.fetchone()[0]
503	
504	
505	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
506	# Helpers
507	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
508	
509	
510	def _row_to_entry(
511	    row: sqlite3.Row, *, last_hit_at_override: float | None = None
512	) -> CacheEntry:
513	    """sqlite3.Row → CacheEntry. `last_hit_at_override` lets
514	    `_get_by_key` reflect the just-written hit timestamp without
515	    a re-SELECT."""
516	    return CacheEntry(
517	        cache_key=row["cache_key"],
518	        workspace_id=row["workspace_id"],
519	        source_url=row["source_url"],
520	        payload=bytes(row["payload"]),
521	        content_type=row["content_type"],
522	        payload_sha256=row["payload_sha256"],
523	        fetched_at=row["fetched_at"],
524	        last_hit_at=(
525	            last_hit_at_override
526	            if last_hit_at_override is not None
527	            else row["last_hit_at"]
528	        ),
529	        fetch_status_code=row["fetch_status_code"],
530	    )

=== react loop turn: cost split between LLM decide vs tool exec ===
    async def _run_react(self) -> AnalysisNotebook:
        """Execute the ReAct loop (legacy mode)."""
        start_time = time.monotonic()
        max_iterations = _MAX_ITERATIONS
        timeout = _TIMEOUT_SECONDS

        logger.info(
            "[react] Starting analysis: %d evidence, max_iter=%d, timeout=%ds",
            len(self._evidence_ids), max_iterations, timeout,
        )

        for iteration in range(1, max_iterations + 1):
            elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time

            # Budget check
            if elapsed >= timeout:
                logger.info(
                    "[react] Timeout reached after %.1fs, stopping", elapsed,
                )
                break

            # Decide next action (retry once on failure)
            decision = None
            for attempt in range(2):
                try:
                    decision = await self._decide(iteration)
                    break
                except Exception as exc:
                    logger.warning(
                        "[react] Decision attempt %d failed at iter %d: %s "
                        "(%s)",
                        attempt + 1, iteration,
                        type(exc).__name__, str(exc)[:200],
                    )
                    if attempt == 0:
                        await asyncio.sleep(1)  # Brief pause before retry

            if decision is None:
                logger.warning(
                    "[react] Decision failed after 2 attempts at iter %d, "
                    "running fallback",
                    iteration,
                )
                await self._run_fallback()
                break

            if decision.action == "stop":
                logger.info(
                    "[react] LLM chose to stop: %s", decision.reasoning[:100],
                )
                break

            # Execute the chosen tool
            step = await self._execute_tool(iteration, decision)
            self._notebook.add_step(step)

            logger.info(
                "[react] Step %d: %s [%s] %.1fs — %s",
                iteration,
                decision.action,
                "OK" if step.result.success else "FAIL",
                step.elapsed_seconds,
                decision.reasoning[:60],
            )

            # Check sufficiency
            if self._is_sufficient():
                logger.info("[react] Sufficient analysis achieved, stopping")
                break

        # If no steps succeeded, run fallback
        if self._notebook.successful_steps == 0:
            logger.warning("[react] No successful steps, running fallback")
            await self._run_fallback()

        # POST-PROCESSING: LLM interprets raw results into real insights
        # This is what separates "regex + scipy" from "analyst with reasoning"
        if self._notebook.successful_steps > 0 and self._client:
            await self._interpret_results()

        total_elapsed = time.monotonic() - start_time

=== tail of speed_investigation.md ===
MEASURED / CODE-VERIFIED EVIDENCE (both worktrees byte-identical — `diff` = IDENTICAL):
- `judge_concurrency.py:55` DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4; `:51-54` memory standard "<=4 agents in
  flight", operators MAY raise "if provider capacity allows". Docstring `:17-24,:31-37` states the
  cap is a faithfulness-STRENGTHENING de-storm: fewer simultaneous mirror-chain POSTs -> fewer 429 ->
  fewer retry-exhaustion fail-CLOSED `('ENTAILED','judge_error')` sentinels -> MORE claims verified
  with the SAME verdicts. Root cause it cures: ~32 simultaneous entailment POSTs 429-stormed the
  `friendli` lead host on the 2026-07-04 preflight, collapsing the abstract/full-verify layers.
- The cap governs THREE verify-path judges only: `entailment_judge.py:155`,
  `credibility_judge_caller.py:143`, `semantic_conflict_detector.py:125` — all in the COMPOSE/verify
  phase, NOT the agentic-retrieval react loop that dominates wall time (~24 of ~30 min). So even in a
  best case it cannot touch the dominant slice.

WHY BOTH DIRECTIONS FAIL:
- RAISE toward 48 (for "speed"): re-introduces the exact 429 storm the cap was built to kill.
  Retry-exhaustion fail-CLOSED drops CHANGE the NLI kept set => violates INVARIANT 2 (faithfulness
  verdict-identity). The storm's 15-50s rate-limit backoffs also burn wall; with
  `PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_WALL_SECONDS_DEFAULT = 900` (outline_agent.py:108) a storm can push the outline
  past the 900s wall => degrade-to-seed => violates INVARIANT 3 (cp4_used=agentic). Note this is a
  DIFFERENT semaphore from the compose-basket-workers(48) that DEADLOCKED at 328-scale, but "48" is
  the same wrong direction.
- LOWER below the band: only SLOWS the verify pass (fewer POSTs in flight). Negative saving. No
  invariant broken, but no benefit — strictly worse.
- The `credibility_pass_concurrency` override (multi_section_generator.py:10157) already raises the
  cap ONLY for the ADVISORY credibility pass (fail-open priors fallback), so even that scoped bump is
  faithfulness-neutral BY CONSTRUCTION and is not a general speed lever for the binding entailment cap.

BOTTOM LINE: the saving is None (the honest claim), and any move made "for speed" (raise) risks
invariants 2 and 3. Default-FALSE applies on both counts: marginal/negative saving AND invariant risk.
Recommendation: pin it in the ~4-8/host band and treat any plan that moves it as a correctness change
requiring a verdict-identity A/B, never as a wall-time optimization.

================================================================================
ADVERSARIAL VERDICT — "Cache fetched BYTES only (retrieval_cache.py), verdict live"
================================================================================
CLAIM: bytes-only per-workspace cache keyed by canon DOI/PMID/URL; strict_verify+NLI
run LIVE on cached content so verdict is recomputed fresh; eviction-on-retraction.
CLAIMED SAVING: run-dependent, largest on re-runs/overlap; ZERO on cold single run.

VERDICT: keeps_quality = FALSE (default-FALSE: saving is ZERO on the target workload
AND carries a residual cross-run faithfulness risk). Details below are code-verified.

FINDING 1 — NOT WIRED TO THE HOT PATH (delivers 0 as shipped).
  `grep -rn 'RetrievalCacheStore(' src` => NO production instantiation; the store is
  constructed ONLY in tests (test_md7_retrieval_cache, test_md7_phase2_cache_warming,
  test_md10_freshness_monitor, test_md3_decision_telemetry).
  The REAL hot fetch path is live_retriever.run_live_retrieval (httpx). It NEVER
  references retrieval_cache / make_cache_key (grep = "NO"). cache_warming.py and
  freshness_monitor.py name the types but are NOT invoked by any orchestration/graph
  entrypoint (only parallel_fetch mentions cache_warming in a docstring). So the M-D7
  cache is effectively dead code on the render path: it saves 0 wall-time today.
  Realizing ANY saving REQUIRES editing live_retriever's fetch loop — forbidden here
  (READ-ONLY; a wheel is live in outline_agent).

FINDING 2 — WRONG SLICE even if wired. The dominant ~24/30 min is the agentic-retrieval
  react loop, serialized "one turn at a time" because each turn's `_decide`
  (react_agent.py:715) is an LLM call that picks the NEXT url from PRIOR results, then
  `_execute_tool` (:743). A bytes cache only shortens the HTTP fetch inside
  `_execute_tool`; it CANNOT shorten the serial LLM-decision latency that is the very
  reason turns are serial. It attacks the fetch sub-slice, not the LLM-dependency chain
  that dominates.

FINDING 3 — ZERO ON THE STATED PROBLEM (proposer's own admission). The target is a
  SINGLE deep render (route_all, 328/329 baskets, ~30 min). Proposer states saving is
  "zero on a cold single run." That IS the workload. Benefit accrues only to
  re-runs/overlapping queries — a different workload than the one under investigation.

FINDING 4 — INTRA-RUN OVERLAP ALREADY PARTLY COVERED. live_retriever already ships two
  perf de-dups that eat most of the "same source fetched by two baskets" gain:
    - versioned authority_enrich.sqlite cache (live_retriever.py:93-116) whose version
      is bumped PRECISELY so cached retracted / boundary-year rows are REBUILT, not
      served stale;
    - a process-local per-URL negative refetch cache (:420, reset each run via
      reset_refetch_cache :434).
  A new raw-bytes cache is largely redundant with these for within-run overlap.

FINDING 5 — RESIDUAL FAITHFULNESS RISK (I2, not fully closed). Verdict recomputed live
  on cached BYTES is only as faithful as the bytes. Cross-run: if a source is retracted
  between runs and the M-D10 freshness daemon does not fire, live NLI runs on stale
  bytes and can KEEP a claim whose real-world evidence was retracted. Eviction
  (evict_by_url) is EVENT-DRIVEN, not guaranteed; pure-TTL is explicitly rejected in the
  header. Note the existing authority_enrich cache solved exactly this by CONTENT-VERSION
  bumping (force-rebuild); a raw-bytes cache has no equivalent content-version guard
  beyond the eviction hook. Bounded but nonzero — proposer acknowledges (I2).

INVARIANT LEDGER: I1 coverage neutral (true). I3 cannot-degrade (true). I4 no-deadlock
  (true — SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE per-call conns, no thread-pool storm). I2 faithfulness:
  verdict-identical ONLY when cached bytes == fresh bytes; stale-across-runs opens a
  residual keep-retracted window mitigated but not eliminated by event-driven eviction.

BOTTOM LINE: On the measured problem (one cold 328-basket render) the saving is ZERO by
  the proposer's own statement, the mechanism isn't even wired to the hot path, and it
  targets the fetch sub-slice rather than the serial-LLM react loop that dominates. That

  Recommendation: do NOT count this as a wall-time optimization for deep renders; if ever
  wired, treat it as a correctness change gated by a full-scale verdict-identity A/B and
